title: Imagined Speech EEG database — Spanish vowels and commands (Pressel et al. 2016)
description: An open-access EEG database of imagined speech recordings from 15 healthy Spanish speakers performing motor imagery tasks involving five vowels and six directional commands. The dataset comprises 6-channel EEG signals sampled at 1024 Hz with preprocessing applied (bandpass 2-45 Hz), containing approximately 278-424 artifact-free trials per subject in total across 11 imagery classes. This resource supports brain-computer interface research and speech imagery decoding studies.
methods_description: EEG signals were recorded from 15 healthy subjects (7 female, 8 male; age 24-28 years) using a Grass 8-18-36 amplifier with DataTranslation DT9816 ADC. Six electrodes (F3, F4, C3, C4, P3, P4) were positioned according to the standard 10-20 montage. Sampling rate was 1024 Hz with online bandpass filtering (2-45 Hz). Subjects performed cue-based motor imagery tasks of five Spanish vowels and six directional commands in response to visual stimuli, with 4-second trial duration. Data were preprocessed with bandpass filtering (2-45 Hz).
